passages.passage_text: (Q. No. 9) In the following question, two statements are given followed by three conclusions. You have to consider the statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts. You have to decide which of the given conclusions if any follow from the given statements.

(Q. Nos. 11-15) Two statements are given followed by two Conclusions I and II. You have to consider the statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts. You are to decide which of the given conclusions, if any, follow from the given statements. Indicate your answer.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements I. Conflicts in mind create tension II. Resolution of conflict leads to good mental health.
Conclusions I. One becomes very hefty and strong by resolving one's conflicts. II. Freedom from conflict leads to good mental health.

choices.c: Neither Conclusion I nor II follows
choices.d: Both Conclusions I and II follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements I. Water has shape. II. The knowledge is like water, flowed from one side to other.
Conclusions I. The knowledge is interdisciplinary II. The knowledge is bound within a specific area. III. The knowledge influences the core of mental activity directly.

choices.c: Both Conclusions I and II follow
choices.d: Both Conclusions I and III follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Examine the following statements I. Only those who have a pair of binoculars become the members of the birdwatcher's club. II. Some members of the birdwatcher's club have participate cameras. III. Those members who have cameras cannot photo-contests.
Which of the following conclusions can be drawn from the above statements?

choices.a: All those who have a pair of binoculars are members of the birdwatcher's club
choices.b: All members of the birdwatcher's club have a pair of binoculars
choices.c: All those who take part in photo-contests are members of the birdwatcher's club

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements A. No teacher comes to the school on a bicycle. B. Anand comes to the school on a bicycle.
Conclusions I. Anand is not a teacher. II. Anand is a student.

choices.a: Conclusion I alone can be drawn
choices.b: Conclusion II alone can be drawn

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement A. Most of the 64 number buses go to my office. B. This is 64 number bus.
Conclusions I. This bus goes to my office. II. This bus does not go to my office.

choices.a: Conclusion I and II both can be drawn
choices.b: Conclusion II alone can be drawn
choices.c: Conclusion I alone can be drawn
choices.d: Conclusions I and II both cannot be drawn

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements A. Vitamin B-complex is the best for health. B. Fruits contain Vitamin B-complex.
Conclusions I. We should grow fruits. II. Fruits are good for health.

choices.a: Only Conclusion I can be drawn
choices.b: Only Conclusion II can be drawn
choices.c: Both Conclusions I and II can be drawn
choices.d: Neither Conclusions I nor II can be drawn

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements A. Some Indians are educated. B. Educated persons like small families.
Conclusions I. All small families are educated. II. Some Indians like small families.

choices.c: Both I and II can be concluded
choices.d: Neither I nor II can be concluded
